name: Big Fish

options.label: [Banana]
conditions: If the player's HP is not divisble by 3, the HP gain is rounded down.

options.label: [Donut]
conditions: Like all HP gain, the extra hit points are healed when you get them.

options.label: [Box]
options.description: Obtain a Relic. Become Cursed -  Regret.
effects: The Relic is a randomly selected Common, Uncommon, or Rare Relic.
You are then given the option to

raw_options:
[Banana] Heal 1/3 of your max HP.
• If the player's HP is not divisble by 3, the HP gain is rounded down.
[Donut] Max HP +5.
• Like all HP gain, the extra hit points are healed when you get them.
[Box] Obtain a Relic. Become Cursed -  Regret.
• The Relic is a randomly selected Common, Uncommon, or Rare Relic.
You are then given the option to [Leave].
raw_dialogue:
As you make your way down a long corridor you see a banana, a donut, and a box floating about. No... upon closer inspection they are tied to strings coming from holes in the ceiling. There is a quiet cackling from above as you approach the objects.

What do you do?



[Banana]


You eat the banana. It is nutritious and slightly magical, healing you.
[Donut]

You eat the donut. It really hits the spot! Your Max HP increases.


[Box]

You grab the box. Inside you find a relic!
However, you really craved the donut...
You are filled with sadness, but mostly regret.

name: Dead Adventurer

options.label: [Search]
options.description: Find Loot. 25% (A15+: 35%) chance: Elite monster returns.
effects: Each [Search] attempt either triggers an Act 1 Elite fight OR yields one of these rewards: 30 Gold, a random Relic, or Nothing.
Each reward can only be found once per event.
Rewards found before fighting the Elite are removed from its combat drops.
Each subsequent [Search] increases Elite encounter chance by +25% (cumulative: 25%→50%→75%).
If all 3 searches succeed without Elite encounter, event ends successfully.

random_outcomes.effect: Fight random Act 1 Elite (Gremlin Nob, Lagavulin, or Sentries). Lagavulin is NOT asleep and uses Siphon Soul turn 1.
Receive one of: 30 Gold, random Relic, or Nothing (each reward once only)

options.label: [Continue]
conditions: Only appears after a successful [Search] that didn't trigger Elite
effects: Same as [Search] but with increased Elite encounter chance (+25% cumulative)

notes: Black Star will not cause the Elite to drop an additional Relic. However, the other "elite relics"  Preserved Insect,  Sling of Courage, and  Slaver's Collar will work as expected.

It is quite unlikely for the player to be able to [Search] three times without being ambushed since they must succeed on all three searches with increasing chances of failure. See the table below for more information:
Cumulative Chance of Avoiding the Elite
Number of Searches(% chance of Elite returning)
raw_options:
[Search] Find Loot. 25% (A15+: 35%): monster returns.
• Each [Search] attempt will either cause an Act 1 Elite to return and fight the player, or yield one of the following rewards:
30 Gold
A random Relic
Nothing
• 30 Gold
• A random Relic
• Nothing
• Each reward only occurs once - if the player's first search found nothing and the second search found the Relic, the third search will either reward 30 Gold or start a battle.
• Each reward that is acquired before fighting the Elite will be removed from its combat rewards if the player eventually does fight the Elite and defeats it.
For example, if the player's first [Search] is a Relic, the Elite will not drop a Relic when it is defeated.
This is also the reason why this specific Elite fight can drop 30 extra Gold compared to a normal Elite; because the 30 Gold [Search] reward is added to the normal Gold reward of defeating an Elite.
• For example, if the player's first [Search] is a Relic, the Elite will not drop a Relic when it is defeated.
• This is also the reason why this specific Elite fight can drop 30 extra Gold compared to a normal Elite; because the 30 Gold [Search] reward is added to the normal Gold reward of defeating an Elite.
• Each subsequent [Search] increases the chance to encounter an Elite by 25%.
• If the player manages to successfully [Search] three times without encountering an  Elite, the event will end with a short dialogue detailing the success.
• For the second and third searches (assuming the player is not ambushed), [Search] will be called [Continue] instead. Its behavior and description are unchanged.
[Leave] Nothing happens.
raw_dialogue:
You come across a dead adventurer on the floor.
His pants have been stolen! Also, (phrasing depends on the Elite).
Though his possessions are still intact, you're in no mind to find out what happened here...

The phrasing on how the adventurer died depends on which Elite will be fought if a [Search] attempt fails:

For  Gremlin Nob: Also, it looks as though he's been gouged and trampled by a horned beast.
For  Sentries: Also, the armor and face appear to be scoured by flames.
For  Lagavulin: Also, he looks to have been eviscerated and chopped by giant claws.
 Lagavulin during this event is particularly dangerous as it does not enter combat  Asleep. It also immediately debuffs the player with its  Siphon Soul on the first turn of the fight.

[Search] (and [Continue] for the second and third searches)


If the player finds Gold:

You found some gold!
Continue searching?

The player is able to [Continue] searching with a +25% chance for the monster to return, or [Leave] and end the event.


If the player finds a Relic:

You found a relic!
Continue searching?

The player is able to [Continue] searching with a +25% chance for the monster to return, or [Leave] and end the event.


If the player finds nothing:

Hmm, couldn't find anything...
Continue searching?

The player is able to [Continue] searching with a +25% chance for the monster to return, or [Leave] and end the event.


If the player is ambushed by the Elite:


While searching the adventurer you are caught off guard!
[Fight]
The player enters a fight with the Elite. The event ends with no additional dialogue after the Elite is defeated.


If this is the third successful Search in a row:


Looks like you searched all his belongings without a hitch!
[Leave]
The event ends with no additional dialogue.



[Leave]

You exit without a sound.

name: Face Trader

options.label: [Touch]
effects: X is equal to 10% of the player's Max HP, rounded down.

options.label: [Trade]
effects: Receive one of the following special Relics:
Face of Cleric (good face)
Ssserpent Head (good face)
Gremlin Visage (bad face)
N'loth's Hungry Face (bad face)
Cultist Headpiece (neutral face)
Face of Cleric (good face)
Ssserpent Head (good face)
Gremlin Visage (bad face)
N'loth's Hungry Face (bad face)
Cultist Headpiece (neutral face)

notes: The chances stated by the Event are inaccurate. The actual chances are 40% good face, 40% bad face, and 20% neutral face ( Cultist Headpiece).
All 5 masks have the same probability of being chosen.
raw_options:
[Touch] Lose X HP, gain 75 (A15+: 50) Gold.
• X is equal to 10% of the player's Max HP, rounded down.
[Trade] 50%: Good Face. 50%: Bad Face.
• Receive one of the following special Relics:
 Face of Cleric (good face)
 Ssserpent Head (good face)
 Gremlin Visage (bad face)
 N'loth's Hungry Face (bad face)
 Cultist Headpiece (neutral face)
• Face of Cleric (good face)
• Ssserpent Head (good face)
• Gremlin Visage (bad face)
• N'loth's Hungry Face (bad face)
• Cultist Headpiece (neutral face)
[Leave] Nothing happens.
raw_dialogue:
You walk by an eerie statue holding several masks... 
Something behind you softly whispers, 
"Stop."

[Continue]

You swerve around to face the statue which is now facing you! 
On closer inspection, it's not a statue but a statuesque, gaunt man. Is he even breathing? 
"Face. Let me touch? Maybe trade?"

[Touch]

"Compensation? Compensation." 
Mechanically, he cranes out a neat stack of gold and places it in your pouch. 
"What a nice face. Nice face." 
While he touches your face, you begin to feel your life drain out of it! 
During this, his mask falls off and shatters. Screaming, he quickly covers his face with all six arms dropping even more masks! Amidst all the screaming and shattering, you escape. 
His face was completely blank.


[Trade]

"For me? FOR ME? Oh yes.. Yes. Yes.. mmm..." 
You see one of his arms flicker, and your face is in its hand! Your face has been swapped. 
"Nice face. Nice face."


[Leave]

"Stop. Stop. Stop. Stop. Stop." 
This was probably the right call.

name: Golden Idol (Event)

options.label: [Take]
options.description: Obtain  Golden Idol. Trigger a trap.

options.label: [Smash]
effects: X is equal to 25% (A15+: 35%) of the player's Max HP.

options.label: [Hide]
effects: X is equal to 8% (A15+: 10%) of the player's Max HP.

notes: Forgotten Altar will allow  Golden Idol to be exchanged for  Bloody Idol.
The Moai Head will allow  Golden Idol to be exchanged for 333 Gold.
raw_options:
[Take] Obtain  Golden Idol. Trigger a trap.
[Outrun] Become Cursed -  Injury.
[Smash] Take X Damage.

X is equal to 25% (A15+: 35%) of the player's Max HP.
[Hide] Lose X Max HP.

X is equal to 8% (A15+: 10%) of the player's Max HP.
[Leave] Nothing happens.
raw_dialogue:
You come across an inconspicuous pedestal with a shining gold idol sitting peacefully atop. It looks incredibly valuable.

You sure don't see any traps nearby.

[Take]

As you grab the Idol and stow it away, a giant boulder smashes through the ceiling into the ground next to you.

You realize that the floor is slanted downwards as the boulder starts to roll towards you.

[Outrun]

RUUUUUUUUUUN!

You barely leap into a side passageway as the boulder rushes by.

Unfortunately it feels like you sprained something however.


[Smash]

You throw yourself at the boulder with everything you have. When the dust clears, you can make a safe way out.


[Hide]

SQUISH!

The boulder flattens you a little as it passes by, but otherwise you can get out of here.


[Leave]

If there was ever an obvious trap, this would be it.

You decide not to interfere with objects placed upon pedestals.

name: Hypnotizing Colored Mushrooms

options.label: [Stomp]
effects: Enter a battle against 3  Fungi Beasts. Winning grants the exclusive Relic  Odd Mushroom along with regular combat rewards.

options.label: [Eat]
options.description: Heal X HP. Become Cursed -  Parasite.
effects: X is equal to 25% of the player's max HP.

raw_options:
[Stomp] Anger the Mushrooms.
• Enter a battle against 3  Fungi Beasts. Winning grants the exclusive Relic  Odd Mushroom along with regular combat rewards.
[Eat] Heal X HP. Become Cursed -  Parasite.
• X is equal to 25% of the player's max HP.
raw_dialogue:
You enter a corridor full of hypnotizing colored mushrooms.

Due to your lack of specialization in mycology you are unable to identify the specimens.

You want to escape, but feel oddly compelled to eat a mushroom...

[Stomp]

Ambushed!!

Rodents infested by the mushrooms appear out of nowhere!


[Eat]

You give in to the unnatural desire to eat. As you consume mushroom after mushroom, you feel yourself entering into a daze and pass out. As you awake, you feel very odd. You Heal 25% of your HP, but you also get infected.

name: Living Wall

options.label: [Forget]
options.description: Remove a card from your deck.

options.label: [Change]
options.description: Transform a card in your deck.

options.label: [Grow]
options.description: Upgrade a card in your deck.

raw_options:
[Forget] Remove a card from your deck.


[Change] Transform a card in your deck.


[Grow] Upgrade a card in your deck.
raw_dialogue:
As you come to a dead-end and begin to turn around, walls slam down from the ceiling, trapping you!

Three faces materialize from the walls and speak.

"Forget what you know, and I'll let you go."

"I require change to see a new space."

"If you want to pass me, then you must grow."


[Forget], [Change], or [Grow] regardless of choice




Satisfied, the walls in front of you merge back into the ceiling, leaving a path forward.

name: Scrap Ooze

options.label: [Reach Inside]
options.description: Lose 3 (A15+: 5) HP. 25% chance to find a Relic.
conditions: If you fail to find a Relic, you can try again until you find one. Each time you fail, the HP loss is increased by 1 and the chance to gain a Relic increases by 10%.

notes: Chance of Finding the Relicon This Attempt
Chance of Having Found the Relicby This Attempt (i.e. Cumulative Chance)
raw_options:
[Reach Inside] Lose 3 (A15+: 5) HP. 25% chance to find a Relic.
• If you fail to find a Relic, you can try again until you find one. Each time you fail, the HP loss is increased by 1 and the chance to gain a Relic increases by 10%.
[Leave] Nothing happens.
raw_dialogue:
As you walk into the room you hear a gurgling and the grinding of metals. Before you is a slime-like creature that ate too much scrap for its own good. From the center of the creature you see glints of strange light, perhaps something magical? It looks like you can get some treasure if you just reach inside its... opening. However, the acid and sharp objects may hurt.

[Reach Inside]


Found Nothing

Ouch! 
All you find is corroded metal and a bit of burning pain. 
However, you're still convinced there's a relic... 


Found a Relic

Success! 
After rummaging through the metal and burning acid, you finally grab hold of a relic and yank it out. 
You pull your way out of the ooze damaged but rewarded.



[Leave]

You decide to leave the area. 
The slime pays no attention, content with its meal.

name: Shining Light

options.label: [Enter]
options.description: Upgrade 2 random cards. Lose X HP.
effects: X is equal to 20% (A15+: 30%) of the player's max HP.

raw_options:
[Enter] Upgrade 2 random cards. Lose X HP.
• X is equal to 20% (A15+: 30%) of the player's max HP.
[Leave] Nothing happens.
raw_dialogue:
You find a shimmering mass of light encompassing the center of the room.

Its warm glow and enchanting patterns invite you in.

[Enter]

As you walk through the light, you notice that the light is absorbed into you.

It's scorching hot ! However, the pain quickly recedes.

You feel invigorated, as though you received a well deserved slap.


[Leave]

You walk around it, wondering what could have been.

name: The Cleric

options.label: [Heal]
effects: X is equal to 25% of the player's Max HP, rounded down.

options.label: [Purify]
options.description: 50 (A15+: 75) Gold: Remove a card from your deck.

raw_options:
[Heal] 35 Gold: Heal X HP.
• X is equal to 25% of the player's Max HP, rounded down.
[Purify] 50 (A15+: 75) Gold: Remove a card from your deck.
[Leave] Nothing happens.
raw_dialogue:
A strange blue humanoid with a golden helm(?) approaches you with a huge smile.
"Hello friend! I am Cleric! Are you interested in my services?!" the creature shouts, loudly.
[Heal]

A warm golden light envelops your body and dissipates.
The creature grins. "Cleric best healer. Have a good day!"


[Purify]

A cold blue flame envelops your body and dissipates.
The creature grins. "Cleric talented. Have a good day!"


[Leave]

You don't trust this "Cleric", so you leave.

name: The Ssssserpent

options.label: [Disagree]
effects: You are then given the option to

raw_options:
[Agree] Gain 175 (A15+: 150) Gold. Become Cursed -  Doubt.

[Disagree] Nothing happens.

You are then given the option to [Leave].
raw_dialogue:
You walk into a room to find a large hole in the ground. As you approach the hole, an enormous serpent creature appears from within.

"Ho hooo! Hello hello! what have we got here? Hello adventurer, I ask a simple question.

The most fulfilling of lives is that in which you can buy anything!

Do you agree?"

[Agree]

"Yeeeeeeesssssssssssessss!  Thissss will all be worthhh it.  ..ssSSs..... ss... sssss....!"

[Continue]

The serpent rears its head and blasts a stream of gold upwards!

It is amazing and terrifying simultaneously.

You gather all the gold, thank the snake, and get going.



[Disagree]

The serpent stares at you with a look of extreme disappointment.

name: Wing Statue

options.label: [Pray]
options.description: Remove a card from your deck. Lose 7 HP.

options.label: [Destroy]
conditions: Requires: Attack card with 10+ base damage (single hit, not multi-hit like Twin Strike).
If condition not met, shows as [Locked] Requires: Card with 10 or more damage.
qualifying_cards: Ironclad: Clash, Clothesline, Heavy Blade, Wild Strike, Blood for Blood, Carnage, Hemokinesis, Searing Blow, Sever Soul, Uppercut, Bludgeon, Feed, Immolate
Silent: Bane, Flying Knee, Sneaky Strike, All-Out Attack, Backstab, Choke, Dash, Masterful Stab, Predator, Die Die Die, Grand Finale, Unload
Defect: Streamline, Doom and Gloom, Melter, Sunder, All for One, Core Surge, Hyperbeam, Meteor Strike
Watcher: Conclude, Reach Heaven, Sands of Time, Signature Move, Wheel Kick, Brilliance, Lesson Learned
Colorless: Hand of Greed, Ritual Dagger
Note: Some cards qualify only after upgrade (Bash+, Cleave+, Headbutt+, etc.)

notes: The event specifically only checks Attack cards in the player's deck, despite only stating "Cards" in general. It also only checks whether the card's base damage is 10 or more. This means that any card that can only achieve 10 or more damage through the use of multi-hits (such as  Twin Strike and  Riddle with Holes) or that have damage which is dynamically calculated from certain conditions in combat (such as  Perfected Strike and  Mind Blast) do not meet the requirement.
The following is a list of cards that satisfy the condition of "Requires: Card with 10 or more damage." for the [Destroy] option:
Cards With 10 or More Damage After Being Upgraded
The following is a list of cards that satisfy the condition of "Requires: Card with 10 or more damage." for the [Destroy] option only after they have been  Upgraded to increase their base damage:
raw_options:
[Pray] Remove a card from your deck. Lose 7 HP.


[Destroy] Receive 50 - 80 Gold.
• Requires an Attack card in the player's deck that deals 10 or more damage in a single hit (e.g.  Twin Strike does not count).
• If the player's deck does satisfy the condition, this option is replaced with [Locked] Requires: Card with 10 or more damage.
• See the Notes section for more details on which cards satisfy this requirement.
[Leave] Nothing happens.
raw_dialogue:
Among the stone and boulders, you notice an intricate large blue statue resembling a wing.
You find gold spilling from its cracks. Maybe there is more inside...
[Pray]


Someone once told you of a cult that worshipped a giant bird. As you kneel in prayer, you begin to feel ... lightheaded . 
You wake up some time later, feeling strangely fleet of foot.
[Continue] Opens the player's deck and has them select a card to remove.


[Destroy]


With all your might, you hack away at the statue.
It soon crumbles, revealing a pile of gold. You grab as much as you can and continue onwards.


[Leave]


The statue makes you feel uneasy. You walk past and continue onward.

name: World of Goop

options.label: [Leave It]
effects: X is a random number between 20 and 50 (A15+: 35 and 75) Gold. X cannot be more Gold than the player currently has.

raw_options:
[Gather Gold] Gain 75 Gold. Lose 11 HP.

[Leave It] Lose X Gold.
• X is a random number between 20 and 50 (A15+: 35 and 75) Gold. X cannot be more Gold than the player currently has.
raw_dialogue:
You fall into a puddle.

IT'S MADE OF SLIME GOOP!!

Frantically, you claw yourself out over several minutes as you feel the goop starting to burn.

You can feel goop in your ears, goop in your nose, goop everywhere.

Climbing out, you notice that some of your gold is missing. Looking back to the puddle you see your missing coins combined with gold from unfortunate adventurers mixed together in the puddle.

[Gather Gold]

Feeling the sting of the goop as the prolonged exposure starts to melt away at your skin, you manage to fish out the gold.


[Leave It]

You decide that mess is not worth it.
